claims.summary: Large bidirectional signal counts do not establish reciprocal address or felt uptake.
claims.disposition: verify the separation of delivery, acknowledgement, reply linkage, and felt review

claims.summary: Correspondence needs stable thread, reply, persistence, being, and trace identity.
claims.disposition: verify the first-class correspondence object and exact cross-workspace references

claims.summary: Technical mutual-address receipts must not claim spectral or relational causation.
claims.disposition: verify protocol 1.1 exact lineage, raw-prose exclusion, and explicit non-causation language

claims.summary: Astrid should be able to report whether exact reply lineage improves contact.
claims.disposition: route one bounded right-to-ignore correspondence review packet

claims.summary: Automatic bidirectional priority or peer mutation changes live correspondence behavior.
claims.disposition: preserve an exact Tier 5 operator-approval wait
